sound_type: loud 'klew-klew-klew' -- ringing, three-note descending call. Carries far. Sentinel alarm. First bird heard at wetlands. E=5
ecosystem: Marshes, tidal flats, mudflats, shallow ponds, flooded fields. Breeds in boreal forest muskegs. Migrant through PNW.
season: Common in PNW spring and fall migration. Some overwinter. Breeds in boreal Canada/Alaska.

coordinates.description: Greater Yellowlegs on PNW marshes and tidal flats. Sentinel species. First to call alarm.

taxonomy_note.description: SIXTH AND FINAL SCOLOPACIDAE. Extends streak to six entries (168-173). Bill morphology circle: straight > decurved > down > extreme > up > straight again. Sentinel species. New genus: Tringa (shanks/yellowlegs).
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Scolopacidae: Wilson's Snipe (168), Dunlin (169), Whimbrel (170), Long-billed Curlew (171), Marbled Godwit (172), Greater Yellowlegs (173).
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Tringa from Greek 'trungas' (sandpiper). melanoleuca from Greek 'melas' (black) + 'leukos' (white) -- black and white plumage. Greater Yellowlegs: larger species with yellow legs. Straightforward.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Tringa -- shanks and yellowlegs. ~12 species worldwide. Medium shorebirds, often vocal, wading in shallow water.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Charadriiformes. Scolopacidae continuing from degrees 168-172. Sixth consecutive entry.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Sixth Scolopacidae. First Tringa. Sentinel species. Bill returns to straight. Circle closes.

song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: ~2500 Hz per note in three-note sequence.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Each three-note 'klew-klew-klew': 0.5-0.8 seconds. Often repeated in series.
song_metrics.note_structure: Three descending notes: 'KLEW-klew-klew.' Stress on first note. Clear, piercing, carrying.
song_metrics.note_types: klew-klew-klew call (primary -- alarm/flight/contact), single 'klew!' (mild alarm), rapid 'kew-kew-kew-kew' (intense alarm), display call (breeding).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Three-note descending pattern. Often repeated: 'klew-klew-klew... klew-klew-klew...' Series of 2-6+ repetitions.
song_metrics.timbre: Clear, ringing, piercing. Carries extraordinary distances for bird size. Cuts through wind and wave noise.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=5. Very loud. Sentinel function amplifies. Community alarm.
song_metrics.tempo: Moderate. Three notes at ~0.5-0.8 sec per sequence. Intensity increases with threat level.
song_metrics.context: Alarm, flight, disturbance, contact. Marshes and tidal flats. Given frequently -- one of the most vocal shorebirds.
song_metrics.key_property: RINGING 'KLEW-KLEW-KLEW' -- sentinel alarm, carries far, triggers community response. Straight bill (circle closes). Yellow legs. E=5. v1.49.313.

plumage.overall: Gray-brown above with white spotting. White below with gray-brown barring/streaking. Breeding: heavier barring on flanks. Bright yellow legs. Straight dark bill.
plumage.diagnostic_mark: Bright yellow legs. Straight bill ~1.5x head length. Gray-brown and white. White rump in flight.
plumage.seasonal_variation: Breeding has heavier flank barring. Non-breeding cleaner below.
plumage.flight_pattern: White rump and tail visible. Dark wings, no wing stripe. Long yellow legs trail behind.

physical_description.overall: Length: 29-33 cm. Weight: 110-230g. Bill: ~5.5 cm, straight, dark. Legs: bright yellow, long. Tall, alert, wary.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Bright yellow legs. Straight bill (longer than head). Tall. Notably wary.
physical_description.field_id: Tall wary shorebird on marshes and flats. Bright yellow legs. Straight dark bill. Listen for loud ringing 'klew-klew-klew' -- often the first bird heard.

nodes.title: Ringing 'Klew-Klew-Klew' -- Sentinel Alarm
nodes.description: Loud, ringing, three-note descending call. 2000-3500 Hz. Carries far. Functions as community alarm -- triggers flight in all shorebirds. E=5.

nodes.title: Sentinel Species -- First to Call
nodes.description: First to detect threats. First to give alarm. Alarm cascades through all shorebirds on the flat. Wariness serves the community. Long legs provide scanning vantage.

nodes.title: Bill Morphology Circle (168-173)
nodes.description: Six Scolopacidae. Bill progression: straight (168) > decurved (169) > down-curved (170) > extremely down-curved (171) > upturned (172) > straight (173). Circle closes.

nodes.title: Six Consecutive Scolopacidae (168-173)
nodes.description: Longest family streak in the engine. Six entries, six bill shapes, six calls, six energies. Closed by the sentinel species.

cross_references.relationship: STREAK SEQUENCE! Godwit upturned (172) to yellowlegs straight (173). Bill morphology circle returns to straight. Both loud tidal flat callers.
SENTINEL ALARM! Greater Yellowlegs' call triggers Dunlin flock flight. One bird's alarm moves thousands. Sentinel (173) protects flock (169).
BILL CIRCLE! Both straight-billed Scolopacidae. Snipe opens streak (168), yellowlegs closes (173). Straight to straight across six entries.
TIDAL FLAT VOICES! Both loud on tidal flats. Curlew: deep probing, rising call. Yellowlegs: sentinel, descending alarm. Different roles, same flat.
